title: Gonolobus ecuadorensis
taxonomy: Gonolobus ecuadorensis is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. It is a type of vine or climber that is native to Ecuador. The plant has been classified within the genus Gonolobus, which belongs to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Further classification places Gonolobus ecuadorensis within the family Apocynaceae, order Gentianales, and kingdom Plantae. Its classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Gonolobus ecuadorensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as a type of milkweed vine. Research has shown that Gonolobus ecuadorensis possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gonolobus ecuadorensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Gonolobus ecuadorensis has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections.
